trigger.from_breadth: An internal srcip with repetitive outbound ACCEPT to a single external dstip (Surface-6 O7), OR run on top-N outbound talkers

telemetry_required: FortiGate action, srcip, dstip, dstport, sentbyte, rcvdbyte, sessionid + per-session timestamps
telemetry_gap_behavior: FortiGate is the best-parsed surface (all 4 live) so data exists; if the device source is missing use devname/policyid fallback. Never infer beaconing from denied sessions only.

method.compute: ordered list of session timestamps + sentbyte + rcvdbyte; total connection count

method.goal: Periodicity test (the math breadth cannot do by eye)
method.compute: inter-arrival deltas between consecutive sessions; mean interval; stddev; jitter = coefficient of variation (CoV) = stddev/mean
method.threshold: CoV < 0.15 = strong beacon (metronomic); 0.15-0.35 = jittered beacon (still suspicious); > 0.35 = not periodic

method.compute: mean + stddev of sentbyte across sessions
method.threshold: small + low-variance payload (consistent check-in size) corroborates beacon; large/variable = likely legit transfer

method.goal: Count gate (exclude trivially-rare and chatty-app noise)

method.decision_input: non-allowlisted + hosting/Tor ASN or odd port = elevate

decision.CRITICAL_conf5: low-jitter periodic external beacon to Tor/known-bad ASN, OR same host has a paired ESET/endpoint detection (cross-surface chain)
decision.HIGH_conf4: CoV < 0.15 + consistent small payload + external non-allowlisted dst = C2
decision.MEDIUM_conf3: CoV 0.15-0.35 jittered pattern to a non-allowlisted dst
decision.FP_kill: dst is Zscaler / Cato / Windows Update / MS telemetry / NTP / known SaaS / internal monitoring collector

false_positive_checks: Zscaler egress 136.226.x / 165.225.x ; Cato egress IPs -> cap, expected periodic
Windows Update / Microsoft telemetry / Office CDN ranges
backup / replication jobs (regular by design) -> confirm dst is a sanctioned backup target
